Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Highly printable, strong, and ductile ordered intermetallic alloy

Composition_Info.Note: Pre-alloyed CCIMA powder fabricated using electrode induction melting gas atomization (EIGA). Powder particle size ranged from 24.2 to 58.2 μm with mean diameter D50 = 37.6 μm.

Process_Text.original: Pre-alloyed CCIMA powder with composition Ni58.48Co18.9Si11.6Ti6.6Al4.4B0.02 (at. %) was fabricated using the electrode induction melting gas atomization (EIGA) technique.
Process_Text.simplified: Electrode induction melting gas atomization (EIGA) powder fabrication.

Microstructure_Text.original: Powder_Characterization: Powder particles exhibit nearly spherical shape. Particle size determined by Mastersizer 3000 laser particle size analyzer.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Powder_Characterization: Powder particles exhibit nearly spherical shape. Particle size determined by Mastersizer 3000 laser particle size analyzer.

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.Powder_Characterization: Powder particles exhibit nearly spherical shape. Particle size determined by Mastersizer 3000 laser particle size analyzer.

Composition_Info.Note: As-printed CCIMA. Nearly fully dense samples obtained when laser scanning speeds varied from 600 to 1000 mm/s.

Process_Text.original: LPBF using SLM 125HL machine equipped with IPG fiber laser (max power 400W, beam diameter 64 μm). Laser power 200W, scanning speed 900 mm/s. Layer thickness 0.03 mm, hatch distance 0.12 mm, laser-beam rotation 67°. Argon protective atmosphere with oxygen content below 400 ppm. 316 L stainless steel substrate heated to 200°C.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF with laser power 200W, scanning speed 900 mm/s, layer thickness 30 μm, hatch spacing 120 μm, 67° rotation, Ar atmosphere.

Microstructure_Text.original: As-printed CCIMA contains dendritic microstructure consisting of brittle G phase. Micro-CT reveals only a few pore defects with low porosity of 0.005%. No micro-cracks detected by micro-CT or optical microscopy. Average pore diameter 22.0 μm.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: As-printed microstructure with G phase dendrites, low porosity 0.005%, crack-free.

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.Micro-CT: Diondo D2 machine with W radiation at 30 μA and 210 kV. Sample rotated 0° to 360° with step size 0.2°, scan resolution 2 μm.
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.SEM: FEI Scios, 20 kV, 3 nA, BSE mode.

Composition_Info.Note: Measured composition shows very small chemical change compared with pre-alloyed powder. B content below SEM-EDS detection limit.

Process_Text.original: LPBF using SLM 125HL machine equipped with IPG fiber laser (max power 400W, beam diameter 64 μm). Laser power 200W, scanning speed 900 mm/s. Layer thickness 0.03 mm, hatch distance 0.12 mm, laser-beam rotation 67°. Argon protective atmosphere with oxygen content below 400 ppm. 316 L stainless steel substrate heated to 200°C. As-printed samples then subjected to single-step thermal stabilization treatment at 1000°C for 2h followed by air cooling.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF (200W, 900 mm/s) followed by thermal stabilization at 1000°C/2h with air cooling.

Microstructure_Text.original: Highly ordered L12-type (Ni,Co)3(Si,Ti,Al) chemically complex superlattice structure. Random grain distribution with fully equiaxed grains and homogeneous elemental distributions. Disordered interfacial nanolayer (DINL) at grain boundaries with ~5 nm thickness, rich in Co and B, depleted in Ni, Si, Ti, Al. DINL identified as disordered FCC solid-solution (γ phase) highly coherent with adjacent ordered L12 grain. Volume fraction of DINL calculated to be 0.08%. Grain interior shows homogeneous compositional distributions without undesired elemental clustering or precipitation at atomic scale.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: L12-type ordered superlattice grains (17.7 μm avg) with disordered FCC nanolayer (~5 nm) at grain boundaries. Co and B co-segregated at grain boundaries. Homogeneous elemental distribution in grain interiors.

Advanced_Quantitative_Features.XRD: L12-type ordered superlattice structure confirmed. No detrimental phases detected.
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.EBSD: Random grain distribution, max texture index 2.56, equiaxed grains ~17.7 μm.
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.TEM: Grain interior with superlattice diffraction spots confirming highly ordered L12 structure.
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.APT: Homogeneous elemental distribution in grain interior. Co and B segregation at grain boundaries forming DINL.
Advanced_Quantitative_Features.AC-STEM: DINL identified as disordered FCC γ phase, highly coherent with ordered L12 grain. Ni and Co occupy face centers (A-type sublattice), Si/Ti/Al occupy vertices (B-type sublattice).

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at 298.15 K; strain rate 1e-3 s^-1; perpendicular to building direction
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM E8, flat dog-bone-shaped tensile samples with gauge length 12.5 mm and cross-section ~3.2 × 3 mm^2

Properties_Info.Note: Thermal-stabilized CCIMA shows tensile strength reaching 1620 MPa.

Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM E8, flat dog-bone-shaped tensile samples with gauge length 12.5 mm and cross-section ~3.2 × 3 mm^2
Properties_Info.Note: Uniform elongation of 35%. Large ductility achieved with high strength.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: Calculated using modified Williamson-Hall method at different deformation strains from 5% to 35%
Properties_Info.Note: Rapid increase in dislocation density during plastic deformation from 5% to 35% strain.
